senses.definition: the deck or interior area of a ship where people can be, or the ship itself as a platform.
examples: The crew found shelter on the shipboard during the storm.
The soldiers were ordered to stay on shipboard until further notice.

senses.definition: plural of shipboard; decks or areas on multiple ships or the collective shipboard parts of a fleet.
examples: The museum displayed artifacts from shipboards of various ages.
Scattered wreckage lay across the shipboards of several vessels.

senses.definition: carried by or attached to a ship; transported by ship.
examples: The cargo was shipborne and arrived after two weeks.
Shipborne personnel were evacuated before the storm.

senses.definition: bound for a ship; destined to be placed on a ship or to depart by sea.
examples: The crates were shipbound to Rotterdam.
Passengers were shipbound and could not disembark at the port.

senses.definition: a boy employed on a ship, historically used for sailors in training or junior crew.
examples: The shipboy carried messages between decks.
In the old navy, shipboys ran errands and learned seamanship.

senses.definition: the process of dismantling ships at the end of their working life for parts or scrap.
examples: Shipbreaking is a hazardous industry in some countries.
Environmental concerns surround shipbreaking practices.

senses.definition: damaged or broken in a ship; destroyed on a ship.
examples: The shipbroken hull could not stay afloat.
After the battle, the vessel appeared shipbroken and ruined.

senses.definition: a person or company that arranges the buying and selling of ships or ship time (employment).
examples: The shipbroker handled the sale of the tanker.
A shipbroker may arrange chartering and insurance terms.

senses.definition: plural of shipbroker; multiple professionals who arrange ship sales or charters.
examples: Shipbrokers help with international tanker trades.
Many shipbrokers work in London and Singapore.

senses.definition: the process or act of building ships; shipbuilding.
examples: The shipbuild sector is recovering after the downturn.
New shipbuild techniques reduce time to delivery.

senses.definition: a person or company that builds ships.
examples: The shipbuilder launched a new carrier.
Shipbuilders in the region specialize in offshore vessels.

senses.definition: plural of shipbuilder; multiple people or companies that build ships.
examples: Shipbuilders around the coast are expanding their facilities.
The shipbuilders collaborated on a new design.

senses.definition: the industry or activity of constructing ships.
examples: Shipbuilding is a major industry in the coastal city.
Advances in shipbuilding have reduced construction times.

senses.definition: buildings or facilities where ships are built; shipyards.
examples: The shipbuildings near the bay house several new projects.
Older shipbuildings have become museums.

senses.definition: the skill or art of building or maintaining ships; nautical craft or knowledge.
examples: He studied shipcraft to understand vessel repairs.
Traditional shipcraft is taught in maritime academies.

senses.definition: historical or less common term; typically unused in modern English; might refer to a festive or ceremonial ship-related token; not widely recognized.
examples: Ancient records mention shipentine in ceremonial contexts.

senses.definition: a docking or mooring device or term used in some dialects; not common in contemporary standard English.
examples: The shipferd secured the vessel to the pier.

senses.definition: a tradesperson who fits and installs components on ships, such as pipes or scaffolding.
examples: The shipfitter attached the piping system.

senses.definition: plural of shipfitter; workers who fit components on ships.
examples: The shipfitters completed their installation ahead of schedule.

senses.definition: a full load or a great amount of a ship's cargo; a large quantity.
examples: The hold was a shipful of timber.
We received a shipful of cargo after the voyage.

senses.definition: plural of shipful; multiple full loads or quantities.
examples: Several shipfuls of coal were unloaded today.

senses.definition: the act of hiring a ship or chartering a vessel.
examples: They secured shiphire for the offshore survey.
The company offers competitive rates for shiphire.

senses.definition: the owner or holder of a ship's title or ownership; could also mean a person who holds a ship's shares.
examples: The shipholder must approve any major sale.
Shipholders are invited to attend the annual meeting.

senses.definition: a person responsible for maintaining a ship's condition or safeguarding cargo; less common term.
examples: The shipkeeper ensured all lifeboats were ready.

senses.definition: a type of wooden planking used to cover walls or ceilings; known for its horizontal grooves.
examples: The cottage walls were covered with white shiplap.
